steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Domine: 'Domine' is a noun in the vocative meaning 'Lord!'

hints.perfect: Perfect 2nd plural would be 'vocavistis' — 'vocatis' is present
hints.future: Future would be 'vocabitis' — 'vocatis' is present indicative

hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to fly)
hints.voveo, vovere, vovi, votum: These are principal parts of 'voveo' (to vow)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are principal parts of 'video' (to see)

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'Dominus' — 'Domine' with -e ending is vocative (direct address)
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'Domino' — 'Domine' is the vocative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'Domini' ends in -i

translation: Why do you call me Lord, Lord?
